thread.section_title: The Sun Nigeria – Voice of the Nation
thread.title: World Orphans Day: Kalu, Yayi , Barau vow to defend children without parents
thread.title_full: World Orphans Day: Kalu, Yayi , Barau vow to defend children without parents

author: Rapheal
title: World Orphans Day: Kalu, Yayi , Barau vow to defend children without parents
text: From Kenneth Udeh, Abuja Senator representing Ogun West, Solomon Olamilekan Adeola; Deputy Senate President, Barau Jibrin and the senator representing Abia North in the Senate, Dr Orji Uzor Kalu, have reaffirmed their pledge to protect the rights of orphans and other vulnerable children in the country, as well as support them to achieve their life aspirations. Joined by other lawmakers, public office holders and distinguished personalities, the lawmakers made the vow in Abuja yesterday when they converged on Abuja’s Maryam Babangida Centre for Women to mark the World Orphans Day. The programme was organised by the CHADASH Empowerment Foundation, a Non-Governmental Organisation dedicated for widows, orphans and the vulnerable founded by Senator Adeola’s wife, Mrs Temitope Adeola. Business, social, political personalities donated cash that ran into millions of naira to support the cause of the Foundation aimed at defending the rights and assisting the 17.5 million Nigerian orphans. Other prominent Nigerians who attended the event included: the Deputy Governor of Bauchi State, Auwal Jatau; Senator Ali Ndume; former Lagos Deputy Governor and serving Senator, Idiat Adebule; Senator Anthony Okorie; Senator Sani Musa ; Senator Ali Sharafadeen; Senator Okey Ezea; Senator Saliu Mustapha; Senator Neda Bernards Imasuen; Senator Reverend Amos Yohana; Senator Sunday Karimi, National Welfare Secretary of the All Progressives Congress, APC, Chief Donatus Nwankpa, among others. APC South-East National Welfare Secretary, Donatus Nwankpa; Senator Anthony Okorie ; Senator Olamilekan Yayi and Senator Orji Uzor Kalu during the roadwalk. •L-R: Deputy Senate President, Senator Barau Jibrin; Chairman Senate Committee on Appropriations, Senator Solomon Adeola (Ogun West ); Deputy governor of Bauchi State, Auwal Jatau; and Chairman, Senate Committee on Privatisation, Senator Orji Uzor Kalu (Abia – North) yesterday during the CHADASH NGO event in Abuja, dedicated to widows and orphans. Harping on the patriotic obligation to tackle the challenges faced by orphans such as poverty , child labour, poor living conditions, lack of access to education and health, Senator Adeola called on Nigerians to rally round the children. The Senate Committee Chairman on Appropriations, who revealed that he is also an Orphan having lost both parents, called for stronger partnerships and focused action to address the challenges facing Nigerian children, especially the orphans. “Nigeria accounts for 17.5 million orphans out of the 153 million globally, which is 11.6 per cent of the world’s orphan population. “‘One Voice’, which is this year’s theme of the World Orphans Day underscores the need to protect and support these children, many of whom live in vulnerable conditions with little to no support. “Orphanage homes are not enough, responsible parents and individuals who are well to do can adopt these special children so that they can nurture and transform their futures for them to achieve their aspirations,” Yayi said. The Ogun West senator emphasised that his zeal to support the foundation is borne out of the fact that from experience he understands that as orphans they usually do not have biological parents to stand up for them when it matters. Also, Senator Kalu who donated magnanimously through his OUK Foundation pin-pointed the challenges of orphans such as loss of livelihood to being out of school, exposure to violence, and poor health and nutrition. The former Abia State governor lamented that many Nigerian children are facing these challenges due to various unfortunate circumstances. “Orphans in Nigeria face many challenges, including poverty, child labour, and lack of access to education and poverty can impact children’s lives in many ways, including inhibiting their aspirations and limiting their opportunities. “Many orphans suffer child labour, in fact, according the National Plan of Action for Orphans and Vulnerable Children (OVC), 39 per cent of children aged 5–14 are involved in child labour. “I am very much concerned with the lack of education, according to statistics approximately 40 per cent of children in Nigeria don’t attend school while so many have dropped out. “Some orphans and vulnerable children sleep under bridges , roads, and it’s dangerous because they are exposed to bad health conditions and can join criminal gangs. “The objective of CHADASH is similar to that of the OUK Foundation and we shall collaborate to put an end to this scourge”, Kalu said. Also speaking, Deputy Senate President Jibrin assured of the commitment of the 10th Senate to defend the rights of children through the Child Rights Act. The Coverner of the programme, Mrs Temitope Adeola appreciated the donors and also announced that the funds will be channelled to different orphanage homes and judiciously monitored. The highpoint of the event was the road walk which saw the dignitaries, children and others around the state capital participating.
